steps.title: Rewrite the equation
steps.content:
Start with:
$$ax+by=b$$
Solve for $y$:
$$by=-ax+b$$
$$y=-\frac{a}{b}x+1$$
This form lets you read the y-intercept and slope directly from the equation.

steps.title: Use 0 < a < b
steps.content: Since $0\lt a\lt b$, the ratio $\frac{a}{b}$ is between $0$ and $1$. Therefore the slope $-\frac{a}{b}$ is negative but between $-1$ and $0$. The y-intercept is exactly $1$.

steps.title: Desmos representative check
steps.content: Pick simple values that satisfy $0\lt a\lt b$, such as $a=1$ and $b=2$. The equation becomes $x+2y=2$, or $y=-\frac{1}{2}x+1$. That graph has the same required features: y-intercept $1$, gentle negative slope, and x-intercept greater than $1$.

steps.title: Confirm the graph
steps.content: The only graph with y-intercept $1$, a negative slope between $-1$ and $0$, and an x-intercept to the right of $1$ is choice C. So C is correct.
